title: trifolium pratense subsp. sativum
intro: Trifolium pratense subsp. sativum, commonly known as red clover, is a subspecies of the clover plant that is widely cultivated for its medicinal and nutritional properties. Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to support cardiovascular health and alleviate menopausal symptoms. The health benefits of red clover include reducing the risk of heart disease, osteoporosis, and certain cancers, while also promoting bone health and immune function. Red clover can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, capsules, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible addition to a health-promoting regimen.
